name: Racing
feel: clean race workshop with real parts on the table
tagline: Build the perfect rig. Skip the $2,000 mistake.
descriptor: DD wheelbases · wheels · pedals · cockpits · ecosystems
intro: Wheelbases, wheels, pedals and cockpits change faster than anyone can keep up with — and half of them don't fit together. The Racing bay checks compatibility, tracks stock, and tells you the honest truth about what's worth your money.

name: Flight
feel: bright avionics bench with labels and measured parts
tagline: From first yoke to full airliner panel.
intro: MSFS 2024 made everyone a pilot again — but a desk full of mismatched throttles, yokes and rudder pedals fights you the whole flight. The Flight bay maps the gear that actually clicks together, from a clean GA setup to a full airliner panel.

name: Space
feel: cockpit controls, mounts, and bindings without fake sci-fi fog
tagline: HOSAS, HOTAS, and the long haul to the stars.
descriptor: HOTAS · flight sticks · throttles · button boxes
intro: Elite, Star Citizen and DCS reward the people who get their controls right — and punish everyone who guessed. The Space bay untangles single-stick vs dual-stick, boutique vs mainstream, and how to build a cockpit you'll never want to leave.

name: Marine
feel: DIY bridge controls, mapped hardware, and practical diagrams
tagline: Take the helm — even when the gear is borrowed.
intro: Marine sim has the smallest dedicated hardware shelf of any discipline — which is exactly why an honest guide matters. The Marine bay shows what enthusiasts actually use: repurposed wheels and throttles, DIY helm builds, and the rare purpose-built gear that's worth it.

name: Golf
feel: bright bay planning with room measurements and gear photos
tagline: From a $500 monitor to a $50,000 room.
intro: A golf sim is the highest-AOV build in the hobby — and the easiest to overspend on. The Golf bay decodes radar vs photometric launch monitors, the room-size and ceiling-height reality nobody warns you about, and how to spend smart at every budget.
